_description: Narrative reference for translation synthesis grounding. Curated from NER extraction + source text. The synthesizer uses this to verify characters, historical facts, and terminology are not fabricated or confused.

book.title: Per la Libertà!
book.subtitle: Dalle mie conversazioni col Conte Carlo di Rudio, complice di Felice Orsini
book.author: Cesare Crespi
book.narrative_frame: Crespi, an Italian journalist in San Francisco, visits the elderly Count di Rudio in Los Angeles and records his life story through extended conversations. Part 1 covers di Rudio's youth, education, the Risorgimento, and the Orsini conspiracy. Part 2 covers the trial, imprisonment, escape, and military career in America.
book.language_note: The 1913 Italian uses accento facoltativo liberally and formal literary register throughout. Crespi narrates in third person with frequent first-person interjections; di Rudio's direct speech is presented in extended quoted passages.

principals.name: Conte Carlo di Rudio
principals.role: Subject of the book. Italian-born count, Risorgimento participant, co-conspirator with Felice Orsini in the 1858 assassination attempt on Napoleon III. Later served in the U.S. Army (7th Cavalry). Living in Los Angeles at time of interviews.
key_facts: Educated at military academies in Austria
Sentenced to death for Orsini conspiracy, commuted to life on Devil's Island
Served in U.S. Civil War and Indian Wars
Present at Battle of Little Bighorn (1876) with 7th Cavalry

principals.name: Cesare Crespi
principals.role: Author and narrator. Italian journalist based in San Francisco. Visits di Rudio in Los Angeles to record his memoirs.
key_facts: Published by Canessa Printing Co., 708 Montgomery St., San Francisco

principals.name: Felice Orsini
principals.role: Italian revolutionary. Led the 1858 assassination attempt on Napoleon III. Central figure in the narrative.
key_facts: Follower of Mazzini, later broke with him
Planned bombing of Napoleon III's carriage at the Opéra, January 14, 1858
Three bombs thrown; Napoleon survived; 8 bystanders killed, ~150 wounded

di_rudio_family.name: Signora Elisa
di_rudio_family.role: Di Rudio's wife. Appears in the framing narrative in Los Angeles.
di_rudio_family.notes: Described as stout, fair hair fading to white, blue eyes, resolute manner

di_rudio_family.name: Italia Adair
di_rudio_family.role: Daughter of di Rudio. Very pale, dark eyes, slender. Recovering from appendicitis surgery.
di_rudio_family.notes: Named in tribute to Italy. Married name Adair.

di_rudio_family.name: Roma Scott
di_rudio_family.notes: Named in tribute to Rome. Married name Scott.

di_rudio_family.name: America Vickery
di_rudio_family.notes: Named in tribute to America. Married name Vickery.

di_rudio_family.name: Samuel
di_rudio_family.role: Italia's son, named after Uncle Sam.
di_rudio_family.notes: Italia wants him to attend West Point.

di_rudio_family.name: Firstborn son
di_rudio_family.role: Di Rudio's firstborn, born in England. Lives in Arizona, explorer.
di_rudio_family.notes: Born during 'tragic moments' (likely the Orsini affair aftermath).

orsini_conspirators.name: Giuseppe Andrea Pieri
orsini_conspirators.role: Co-conspirator. Accompanied Orsini in the bombing.

orsini_conspirators.name: Simon Bernard
orsini_conspirators.role: Co-conspirator. Made the bombs in London. Tried in England and acquitted.

historical_figures.name: Giuseppe Mazzini
historical_figures.role: Italian revolutionary leader, founder of Giovine Italia. Ideological inspiration for the conspirators.

historical_figures.name: Giuseppe Garibaldi
historical_figures.role: Italian general and patriot. Leader of the Expedition of the Thousand.

historical_figures.name: Napoleon III
historical_figures.role: Emperor of France. Target of the Orsini assassination attempt.

historical_figures.name: Fortunato Calvi
historical_figures.role: Italian patriot and martyr. Portrait hangs in di Rudio's house. Executed by Austria in 1855.

historical_figures.name: Vittorio Emanuele II
historical_figures.role: King of Sardinia, later first King of unified Italy.

historical_figures.name: Josef Radetzky
historical_figures.role: Austrian field marshal. Commanded Imperial forces in northern Italy.

historical_figures.name: Daniele Manin
historical_figures.role: Leader of the Venetian Republic of 1848-49.

historical_figures.name: Presidente Delangle
historical_figures.role: Presiding judge at the Orsini trial.

secondary_characters.name: Fumagalli
secondary_characters.role: Appears in di Rudio's narrative. Context TBD.

secondary_characters.name: Rossi
secondary_characters.role: Appears in di Rudio's narrative. Context TBD.

secondary_characters.name: Morbioli
secondary_characters.role: Appears in di Rudio's narrative. Context TBD.

secondary_characters.name: Giustiniano
secondary_characters.role: Appears in di Rudio's narrative. Context TBD.

secondary_characters.name: Domenico
secondary_characters.role: Appears in di Rudio's narrative. Context TBD.

secondary_characters.name: Trolli
secondary_characters.role: Appears in di Rudio's narrative. Context TBD.

secondary_characters.name: Egassy
secondary_characters.role: Appears in di Rudio's narrative. Context TBD.

historical_events.name: Orsini assassination attempt
historical_events.location: Rue Le Peletier, Paris (outside the Opéra)
historical_events.description: Three bombs thrown at Napoleon III's carriage. Emperor survived. 8 killed, ~150 wounded. Led to trial and execution of Orsini, Pieri, and Gomez. Di Rudio sentenced to death, commuted to Devil's Island.

historical_events.name: Five Days of Milan
historical_events.description: Popular uprising against Austrian rule in Milan.

historical_events.name: Venetian Republic (1848-49)
historical_events.description: Short-lived republic under Manin, ended by Austrian siege.

historical_events.name: Coup d'état of Louis-Napoléon
historical_events.description: Louis-Napoléon Bonaparte seized power, becoming Napoleon III. Motivated Italian revolutionaries' hostility toward him.

historical_events.name: Battle of Little Bighorn
historical_events.description: Di Rudio served with 7th Cavalry under Custer. Survived the battle.

france: Île du Diable (Devil's Island, French Guiana)

political.Risorgimento: The Italian unification movement (1815-1871). Do NOT translate — keep as 'Risorgimento' in italics.
political.Giovine Italia: Young Italy, Mazzini's revolutionary society. Keep in Italian with gloss on first use.
political.colpo di Stato: Coup d'état. Use the French term as standard English.

military.Bersaglieri: Elite Italian infantry corps. Keep in Italian.

legal.Conciergerie: Paris prison where Orsini conspirators were held. Keep as 'Conciergerie'.
legal.La Roquette: Paris prison for those awaiting execution. Keep as 'La Roquette'.

forms_of_address.Signora: Keep as 'Signora' in dialogue, or use 'Mrs.' in narration as appropriate to context
